sections.title: A: Use the word given in capitals to form a word
sections.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap.

items.sentence: I saw some badminton on TV and found it quite (1) ______ (INTEREST).
... so I thought I'd see if there was a club or (2) ______ (ASSOCIATE) in my area.
I looked everywhere but, (3) ______ (FORTUNE), the nearest club was 80 kilometres away.
So, without any (4) ______ (KNOW), I decided to form my own club.
It was very hard in the beginning - I didn't have any (5) ______ (EQUIP).
... and I had to convince a few people that it was an (6) ______ (ENJOY) way to keep fit.
I organised a small (7) ______ (COMPETE), which was a lot of fun ...
Now we've got a full-time (8) ______ (TRAIN) ...
... and our (9) ______ (OPPOSE) come from all over the country.
One of our members was even a bronze (10) ______ (MEDAL) in the national championship!
11 I refuse to put
items.match: up with people who cheat at games.

items.sentence: 12 The current champion was knocked
items.match: out in the second round of the competition.

items.sentence: 13 I used to love basketball, but I've gone
14 The trainer asked us to carry
items.match: on with the activity while he answered the phone.

items.sentence: 15 The organisers had to bring
items.match: the race forward by a week.

items.sentence: 16 You should think about taking
items.match: up a martial art, to defend yourself.

items.sentence: 17 Although she was very young, Olivia took
items.match: to swimming as soon as she tried it.

items.sentence: 18 Rob had to pull
items.match: out of the race because of a last minute injury.

sections.title: C: Complete each second sentence using the word given
sections.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words in each gap.

items.sentence:
Our tennis match started at one o'clock and we were still playing at four.
been
At four o'clock, we ______ three hours.

Terry never used to spend so much time playing on his computer.
use
Terry ______ spend so much time playing on his computer.

Before we play, check all the cards are there.
certain
Before we play, ______ all the cards are there.

Hang-gliding can be dangerous, but after a while you don't mind it.
used
Hang-gliding can be dangerous, but after a while you ______ it.

I ran fifteen miles, and then my trainer told me to do fifty push-ups!
already
When my trainer told me to do fifty push-ups, I ______ fifteen miles!

We wouldn't spend so much time indoors when I was young.
used
We ______ so much time indoors when I was young.

I spend a lot of my time on my hobbies.
up
My hobbies ______ a lot of my time.

I would rather play ludo than snakes and ladders.
prefer
I ______ ludo rather than snakes and ladders.

I don't think it's likely that you'll win the competition.
chance
I think there's ______ the competition.
'Why were you so tired yesterday?' 'Because I ______ all morning.'
It was the first time I ______ a live match.
'You live in a huge house, don't you?' 'Yes, but we ______.'
I ______ the whole of War and Peace by the time I was seven years old.
Karate hurt my hands at first, but I finally ______ it in the end.
I ______ for the match to begin when suddenly a dog ran onto the pitch.
Before she retired, my grandma ______ go for a run every morning before work.
The ______ in the stadium all cheered the athletes.
I got a new baseball ______ yesterday.
After a hard match, United managed to ______ with City, 1-1.
In this game, the players ______ it in turns to throw the dice.
It's nice to win, but the important thing is to ______ your best.
Just ask them if you can play and I'm sure they'll let you ______.
Everyone expects Johnson to ______ Smith in today's final.
